landing.heroSubtitle: The all-in-one platform for community savings groups, alumni unions, village associations, and church groups across Africa and the diaspora
landing.trustedBy: Trusted by community leaders across Africa and the diaspora
landing.featuresSubtitle: Built by Africans, for African community groups. We understand how njangis, tontines, and associations actually work.
landing.featureMembershipDesc: Track members, roles, and standing. Proxy management for elders. One account, unlimited groups.
landing.featureContributionsDesc: Record dues, donations, and savings rotations. Auto-reminders for outstanding balances. Full transparency.
landing.featureMeetingsDesc: Schedule meetings, record attendance, capture decisions and action items. Searchable history.
landing.featureCommsDesc: Announcements, notifications, and group messaging. Reach members via app, email, or SMS.
landing.featureFinanceDesc: Real-time dashboards, treasurer reports, and audit trails. See exactly where every franc goes.
landing.featureBilingualDesc: Full English and French support. Each member chooses their language. Perfect for Cameroon and beyond.
landing.savingsFeatureDesc: Whether you call it Njangi, Ajo, Susu, Stokvel, Chama, or Sou-sou, we've got you covered. Track contributions, rotations, and collections with full transparency.
landing.electionsFeatureDesc: Run officer elections with anonymous ballots, pass motions, and conduct polls. Fair, transparent, and auditable.
landing.documentFeatureDesc: Store your constitution, financial statements, and meeting documents securely. Version-controlled and always accessible.
landing.memberCardFeatureDesc: QR-coded digital cards that verify membership instantly. Download, share, and present with pride.
landing.builtForYouDesc: VillageClaq serves community groups in every corner of Africa and wherever the diaspora calls home.
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: Get your group up and running in minutes
landing.step1Desc: Set up in 30 seconds. Choose your group type, currency, and meeting schedule.
landing.step2Desc: Share a link or send invitations. Members join with one tap.
landing.step3Desc: Record contributions, take attendance, capture minutes. Everything in one place.
landing.pricingSubtitle: Start free. Upgrade when your group grows.
landing.testimonial1Quote: VillageClaq transformed how our group manages finances. Every contribution is tracked, every meeting documented. Total transparency.
landing.testimonial2Quote: We moved from WhatsApp chaos to organized management overnight. Our members love the contribution tracking and automated reminders.
landing.testimonial3Quote: The rotating savings tracker is exactly what we needed. No more spreadsheets, no more arguments about who paid what.
landing.testimonial4Quote: Managing 200+ members across three chamas used to be a nightmare. Now it takes minutes. The reports alone are worth it.
landing.testimonial5Quote: Our stokvel finally has a proper system. Members check their balances anytime, and our treasurer sleeps better at night.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Join 500+ community groups across 11 countries already using VillageClaq to stay connected and accountable.
landing.footerTagline: Bringing African communities together, one group at a time.
landing.footerBuiltWith: Built with love in Cameroon for the world
landing.painPointsSubtitle: Every community group faces these problems. VillageClaq solves them all.
landing.painPoint1Problem: Cash payments with no receipts. Year-end reconciliation becomes a shouting match.
landing.painPoint1Solution: VillageClaq records every payment digitally with instant receipts.
landing.painPoint2Problem: Minutes on someone's phone. Attendance on a sign-in sheet. Contribution records in WhatsApp.
landing.painPoint2Solution: VillageClaq puts everything in one place.
landing.painPoint3Problem: 'I hosted three times but Emeka only hosted once.' Sound familiar?
landing.painPoint3Solution: VillageClaq tracks hosting fairly with a transparent rotation system.
landing.painPoint4Problem: When the treasurer changes, institutional memory disappears.
landing.painPoint4Solution: VillageClaq preserves your group's complete history forever.
landing.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about VillageClaq
landing.faq1Q: What types of groups can use VillageClaq?
landing.faq1A: Njangis, alumni unions, village associations, church groups, family meetings, stokvels, chamas, ajos, susu groups — any community organization.
landing.faq2A: Yes! VillageClaq is fully bilingual in English and French. Every screen, notification, and report is available in both languages.
landing.faq3Q: What if members don't have smartphones?
landing.faq3A: Admins can add proxy members without email or phone. They are fully tracked in the system with all their payments and attendance.
landing.faq4Q: How secure is our financial data?
landing.faq4A: We use Supabase with Row Level Security. Each group's data is completely isolated. All connections are encrypted.
landing.faq5A: Yes! The Free plan supports up to 25 members with full basic features. No credit card required.
landing.faq6A: 18 currencies including XAF, XOF, NGN, GHS, KES, ZAR, USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, and more.
landing.pricingPageSubtitle: Start free. Upgrade when you're ready. No hidden fees.

contact.faq1Q: How do I create a group?
contact.faq1A: Sign up for free, then click 'Create Group' from your dashboard. Setup takes under 30 seconds.
contact.faq2A: Go to your group settings and share the invitation link, or send email invitations directly from the app.
contact.faq3A: Yes. We use Supabase with row-level security, encrypted connections, and your data is never shared with third parties.
contact.faq4A: VillageClaq is free for groups up to 25 members. Paid plans start at $9/month for larger groups.

about.title: About VillageClaq
about.story: Built by Africans, for African Communities
about.storyDesc: VillageClaq was born from a simple observation: millions of Africans manage community finances through trust and paper. We're bringing transparency, efficiency, and pride to every savings circle, alumni union, and village association — from Bamenda to Brooklyn.
about.missionDesc: To empower every African community group with world-class financial management tools, preserving the traditions of mutual aid while building trust through transparency.
about.teamDesc: A passionate team building the future of community finance in Africa.
about.panAfricanDesc: VillageClaq serves communities in every corner of Africa and wherever the diaspora calls home. Whether you run a Njangi in Cameroon, an Ajo in Nigeria, a Susu in Ghana, a Stokvel in South Africa, or a Chama in Kenya — VillageClaq speaks your language.
about.problemDesc: Across Africa, community groups manage millions in contributions using paper notebooks, WhatsApp screenshots, and cash in envelopes. When records are unclear, disputes arise and trust erodes — the very foundation these groups are built on.
about.solutionDesc: One platform for members, payments, attendance, meetings, and reports. Transparent, organized, and accessible from any phone — so every franc is accounted for and every member feels included.
about.valueTransparencyDesc: Every transaction visible. Every decision documented. Trust through openness.
about.valueCommunityDesc: We build for the group, not the individual. Every feature strengthens the collective.
about.valueAfricaDesc: We understand African communities because we are one. Multi-currency, multilingual, mobile-first.
about.valueSimpleDesc: If a village elder can't use it on a basic phone, we haven't finished building it.
about.ctaSubtitle: Start free. No credit card required.

bodies.acceptance: By accessing or using VillageClaq, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our platform.
bodies.services: VillageClaq provides a multi-tenant SaaS platform for African community groups to manage contributions, events, attendance, and group finances. We reserve the right to modify or discontinue any feature at any time.
bodies.accounts: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You must provide accurate information during registration and keep your profile up to date.
bodies.conduct: You agree not to use VillageClaq for any unlawful purpose, to harass other users, to distribute malware, or to attempt to gain unauthorized access to other accounts or systems.
bodies.content: You retain ownership of content you post on VillageClaq. By posting content, you grant us a non-exclusive license to display and distribute that content within the platform.
bodies.privacy: Your use of VillageClaq is also governed by our Privacy Policy. Please review it to understand how we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
bodies.payments: Certain features may require a paid subscription. All fees are non-refundable unless otherwise stated. We may change pricing with 30 days notice to existing subscribers.
bodies.termination: We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms. You may delete your account at any time through your profile settings.
bodies.liability: VillageClaq is provided on an 'as is' basis. We shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the platform.
bodies.governing: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Maryland, United States. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Maryland.
bodies.changes: We may update these terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via email or in-app notification. Continued use constitutes acceptance of updated terms.
bodies.contact: If you have questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at legal@villageclaq.com or through our contact form.

bodies.collection: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email address, phone number, and profile details. We also collect usage data, device information, and cookies when you interact with our platform.
bodies.usage: We use your information to provide and improve our services, process transactions, send notifications, and communicate with you about your account and groups.
bodies.retention: We retain your personal data for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide services. You may request deletion of your data at any time.
bodies.rights: You have the right to access, correct, export, or delete your personal data. You may also object to processing or request restriction of processing.
bodies.cookies: We use essential cookies to keep you logged in and remember your preferences. We use analytics cookies to understand how our platform is used. You can manage cookie preferences in your browser settings.
bodies.thirdParty: We use trusted third-party services including Supabase for data storage, Vercel for hosting, and payment processors for transactions. These services have their own privacy policies.
bodies.security: We implement industry-standard security measures including encryption in transit and at rest, row-level security policies, and regular security audits to protect your data.
bodies.children: VillageClaq is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us.
bodies.changes: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes via email or in-app notification at least 30 days before they take effect.
bodies.contact: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@villageclaq.com or through our contact form.

help.title: Help Center
help.subtitle: Find answers to common questions and learn how to use VillageClaq
help.noResultsDesc: Try adjusting your search or browse a different category.
help.faqEmptyDesc: Check back later — FAQs will be published here.
help.guidesSubtitle: Learn how to use VillageClaq's key features
help.guide1Desc: Learn what standing statuses mean and how they affect your membership.
help.guide1Content:
Every member has a standing status: Good, Warning, or Suspended. Standing is automatically calculated based on four factors:

1. Payment history — overdue contributions cause a standing drop
2. Attendance — below 60% attendance in the last 12 months triggers a warning
3. Relief contributions — falling behind on enrolled relief plans affects standing
4. Open disputes — unresolved disputes cause a soft warning

To improve your standing: pay outstanding obligations, attend meetings regularly, and resolve any pending disputes. Admins can view detailed standing breakdowns on each member's profile page.
help.guide2Desc: How treasurers record payments and what happens after.
help.guide2Content:
Treasurers and authorized officers record payments from the Contributions page. For each obligation, click 'Record Payment' and enter the amount, payment method, and optional reference number.

After recording:
• The obligation status updates (partial or paid)
• The member's standing is recalculated automatically
• A receipt is generated that members can view in their payment history
• An audit log entry is created for accountability

Payments can be recorded as cash, mobile money, bank transfer, or other methods. Partial payments are supported — the system tracks remaining balances.
help.guide3Desc: Understanding contribution types, obligations, and the payment matrix.
help.guide3Content:
Contribution types define what members owe — for example, monthly dues, annual levies, or special assessments. Each type has a name, amount, currency, and frequency (weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly, or one-time).

When a contribution type is created with 'Enroll all members' enabled, obligations are automatically generated for every active member. The Year-over-Year (YoY) matrix shows a visual overview of all members' payment status across periods — green for paid, yellow for partial, red for unpaid.

Admins can create, edit, and manage contribution types from the Contributions admin page.
help.guide4Desc: How mutual aid funds work — enrollment, contributions, and claims.
help.guide4Content:
Relief plans are mutual aid funds where members contribute regularly and receive payouts when qualifying life events occur (e.g., bereavement, medical emergency, wedding).

How it works:
1. Admins create a relief plan with covered events and payout amounts
2. Members enroll in active plans
3. When an event occurs, the member submits a claim with supporting documentation
4. Admins review and approve/deny the claim
5. Approved claims receive payouts recorded by the treasurer

Eligibility may depend on enrollment duration and contribution history. Check your group's specific plan rules.
help.guide5Desc: How meetings and events are tracked, and how attendance affects standing.
help.guide5Content:
Group events include regular meetings, socials, fundraisers, and special events. Admins create events with date, time, location, and type.

Attendance is recorded by officers — members are marked as present, late, absent, or excused. Attendance directly affects your standing: below 60% attendance rate in the last 12 months triggers a warning.

Members can view their own attendance record and rate from the My Attendance page. Excused absences (approved by an admin) do not count against your attendance rate.
help.guide6Desc: How meeting hosting rotation works and fairness scoring.
help.guide6Content:
Many community groups rotate hosting responsibilities for meetings among members. The hosting roster tracks whose turn it is to host.

The system suggests fair rotation based on:
• How recently each member last hosted
• Total hosting count
• Member availability and preferences

Admins manage the roster by creating hosting rosters, adding assignments, and marking them as completed or missed. Missed hosting assignments may affect standing depending on group rules.
help.guide7Desc: Different ways to add members — links, QR codes, manual add, and proxy members.
help.guide7Content:
There are several ways to add members to your group:

1. Share Link — generate an invitation link that anyone with the link can use to join
2. QR Code — display a QR code that members scan with their phone camera
3. Manual Invite — enter an email or phone number to send a direct invitation
4. Proxy Members — create a membership for someone without a smartphone or account (elderly members, non-tech users). Proxy members are managed by the admin who created them.

All new members go through an acceptance flow. Admins can set the default role for new members.
help.guide8Desc: How to apply for group loans, eligibility, and repayment.
help.guide8Content:
Groups can enable a loan program where members borrow from group funds. The loan process:

1. Admin configures loan rules (max amount, interest rate, repayment period, eligibility)
2. Eligible members apply for a loan specifying the amount and purpose
3. Admins review and approve/deny the application
4. Approved loans are disbursed and a repayment schedule is generated
5. Members make monthly repayments tracked by the system

Eligibility may require minimum membership duration, good standing, and no existing active loans. Interest rates and maximum amounts are configured per group.
help.guide9Desc: Understanding fines, how to dispute them, and the resolution process.
help.guide9Content:
Admins can issue fines to members for rule violations (late payments, missed meetings, etc.). Each fine has an amount, reason, and due date.

If you believe a fine was issued in error, you can dispute it:
1. Go to My Fines and click 'Dispute' on the fine
2. Provide your reason and any supporting documents
3. An admin will review the dispute and either resolve it in your favor (waiving the fine) or dismiss it

Unpaid fines affect your member standing. Fines can also be waived by admins with a recorded reason.
help.guide10Desc: How organizations with headquarters and branches work.
help.guide10Content:
VillageClaq supports multi-level organizations where a headquarters (HQ) group oversees branch groups. This is useful for large associations with regional chapters.

Key features:
• HQ admins can view aggregated data across all branches
• Members can be transferred between branches
• Each branch maintains its own finances, events, and membership
• Shared contribution types can be pushed from HQ to all branches

This feature is available on enterprise plans. Contact support to set up your organization structure.

changelog.v100_description: VillageClaq is your all-in-one platform for managing community groups — from finances to meetings, relief funds to elections.
changelog.v100_feature1: Track member contributions with automated obligations and payment recording
changelog.v100_feature2: Manage events, attendance, and hosting rosters with fair rotation
changelog.v100_feature3: Set up mutual aid relief plans with claim submissions and payouts
changelog.v100_feature4: Run a group loan program with eligibility rules and repayment tracking
changelog.v100_feature5: Issue and manage fines with a built-in dispute resolution process
changelog.v100_feature6: Enterprise support with HQ/branch structure and cross-group reporting

helpTips.contributionTypes: Contribution types define what members owe. Each type has a name, amount, and frequency. Obligations are auto-generated for enrolled members.
helpTips.reliefPlans: Relief plans are mutual aid funds. Members contribute regularly and receive payouts when qualifying life events occur.
helpTips.yoyMatrix: Year-over-Year payment matrix. Green = paid in full, Yellow = partial, Red = unpaid. Each column represents a period.
helpTips.hostingRoster: The hosting roster tracks which members host group meetings. The system suggests fair rotation based on hosting history.
helpTips.loanConfig: Configure how loans work for your group: maximum amounts, interest rates, eligibility requirements, and repayment terms.
helpTips.fineTypes: Define the types of fines your group uses. Fines can be issued manually by admins for rule violations.
helpTips.memberStanding: Standing is auto-calculated from payment history, attendance rate, relief contributions, and open disputes.
helpTips.myStanding: Your standing reflects whether you're up to date with payments, attendance, and other group obligations.
